reasoning: The current 4-week moving average (229,250) is relatively high compared to recent history. The FRED statistical baseline indicates a 32% chance of an increase in the near term because of mean reversion. However, the moving average calculation for May 17 will drop the week of April 19, which appears to have been a relatively low week for initial claims, which adds some upward pressure to the moving average for the first resolution date. Historically (e.g. 2024), claims tend to peak over the summer (reaching 237k in July/August), thus favoring an increase by August. Toward November, seasonal effects often balance out and baseline uncertainty is maximal. The probabilities are structured so that uncertainty strictly increases with the forecast horizon (i.e. strictly tending toward 0.50 as the horizon extends).
